why do you want to work at discord?: Discord sits at a fascinating intersection of large-scale frontend infrastructure and a platform that genuinely shapes how millions of people connect around gaming. The Web Platform team's work — owning build systems, CI/CD pipelines, and developer tooling that hundreds of engineers rely on daily — is exactly the kind of high-impact, foundational engineering I find most compelling. I've spent the last four years building scalable backend services and frontend applications at Vanguard and Xebia, and I'm eager to bring that experience to a team where the 'product' is developer productivity itself. The challenge of optimizing JavaScript build tooling, improving deployment reliability, and enabling engineers to ship faster at Discord's scale is a genuinely exciting engineering problem. Beyond the technical work, Discord's role in the gaming ecosystem means the infrastructure improvements this team makes have a direct, tangible effect on millions of daily users — that kind of reach is motivating.
why do you want to join figma?: I'm drawn to Figma because of the opportunity to work at the intersection of data infrastructure, machine learning, and product — an area I'm genuinely passionate about. At Vanguard, I've built AI-powered backend services integrating LLM APIs for intelligent text processing and automated workflows, and I've engineered event-driven, high-throughput data pipelines on AWS. Figma's mission to make design accessible to all, combined with its investment in building a next-generation ML and data platform, is exactly the kind of challenge I want to be part of. The chance to be an early engineer shaping foundational systems — from feature pipelines and model serving to self-serve analytics — aligns closely with the scalable, AI-driven backend work I've been doing. I'm excited by the prospect of bringing data and models closer to the product experience at a company that's redefining how teams collaborate and create.
additional information: I'm excited about this opportunity at Figma because it sits at the intersection of data infrastructure, ML systems, and product impact — areas I'm deeply passionate about. At Vanguard, I've designed AI-powered backend services integrating Python-based LLM APIs for intelligent text processing and automated workflows, and built event-driven pipelines using DynamoDB Streams, Lambda, and SNS/SQS achieving 99.99% reliability. At Xebia, I built scalable microservices, automated infrastructure with Terraform on AWS and GCP, and implemented Kafka-based event-driven architectures that reduced inter-service failures by 35%. The prospect of shaping Figma's ML and data platform — from feature pipelines and model serving to prompt-processing systems and self-serve analytics — aligns directly with the work I find most energizing. I hold an MS in Computer Science from UNC Charlotte, am currently on F-1 OPT, and am open to working remotely or relocating for the right opportunity. I'd love to bring my full-stack and data engineering background to help Figma make AI a first-class part of its product experience.
